Field Trip Report · Perception Sketching

Forest Research Institute
A Visit to Remember

Half a day of sketching, photography, and quiet observation

DurationHalf Day
StructureGuided Tour
FocusArchitecture & Photography
LocationFRI, Dehradun

01

Overview

After the Rishikesh trip, we visited the Forest Research Institute in Dehradun for a half-day session. The purpose was to observe and sketch the architecture, and to document it through photography. The visit was structured as a guided tour across the campus.

02

Places we visited

We covered the main building, the museum inside it, and a nature trail on the campus grounds.

📍 Main Building 📍 Museum Interiors 📍 Nature Trail

03

What we sketched and photographed

Most of our attention went to the main building and the museum. The building has large columns and arches in a Greco-Roman style which gave a lot to observe and draw. Inside the museum, the wooden ceilings, walls, and display cases had a lot of fine detail that made for interesting sketching subjects.

Sketching a heritage building is much harder than sketching outdoors. Every column and arch has specific proportions — one wrong line and the whole thing looks off. It required a lot more patience and careful looking than our Rishikesh sessions.

04

Experience

Coming straight from Rishikesh, the first thing we noticed at FRI was how quiet it was. No crowds, no noise from the river — just the sound of birds and the wind through the trees. It made sketching feel much calmer and more focused.

The campus is very large and beautiful. The old buildings surrounded by dense forest give it a very different feel from anything we had seen before. The nature trail had a pond which was the only water we came across the entire visit.

05

Challenges

Half a day was simply not enough. The campus is huge and there was so much more we could have sketched and explored. We had to move quickly from place to place which made it hard to sit and properly observe the details.

There was also nothing to eat or drink the whole time. The canteen was under construction, and we could not find any water cooler, filter, or drinking fountain anywhere on campus. The only water we saw was the pond on the nature trail.

💧

Worth noting

It felt strange that a forest research institute — a place all about nature — had no accessible water source for visitors. Something as simple as a water cooler would make a big difference.

06

Conclusion

The FRI visit was a very different experience from Rishikesh — quieter, more structured, and more focused on detail. Sketching inside a heritage building taught us a new kind of patience and observation. Despite the short time and lack of basic facilities, it was a memorable visit and the campus left a strong impression on all of us.

Perception Sketching — Field Trip Report Forest Research Institute, Dehradun
